commit.message:
Fix _ensure_vendors_table: use PRAGMA to detect missing columns

The previous bare ALTER TABLE approach may have silently failed.
Now queries PRAGMA table_info to get existing columns first, then
only ALTERs for truly missing ones. Uses query() for proper row
parsing and caches with _vendors_ensured flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: unwrap blockquotes inside list items for LK schema compatibility

LK's listItem schema only allows paragraph, bulletList, orderedList,
mediaSingle, codeBlock, extension, and bodiedExtension as children.
Blockquotes nested inside list items are now unwrapped into their
inner paragraphs to avoid import validation errors.

fix: auth system audit — persistent sessions on Railway, rate limiting, SESSION_SECRET validation

- Remove Railway MemoryStore exception: sessions now persist across deploys using
  PostgreSQL session store backed by the existing SSL-configured pool
- Use existing db pool for connect-pg-simple to avoid ECONNRESET (pool already
  has ssl.rejectUnauthorized=false for Railway)
- Fix connect-pg-simple TTL units: pass seconds not milliseconds
- Fix resave/saveUninitialized: both false (was true, causing unnecessary DB writes)
- Add SESSION_SECRET validation with loud warning + random fallback if unset
- Add auth-specific rate limiters before general limiter (login: 10/15min,
  signup: 5/hr, forgot-password: 5/hr)
- Add OAuth startup logging showing all provider config status and callback URL
- Fix encoding corruption in oauth.ts comment

repository.description: AI-powered B2B sourcing platform connecting buyers with verified global suppliers. Features intelligent supplier discovery, landed cost calculator, risk assessment, and automated RFQ management.

commit.message:
Add extrapolation, player timeout, and secure API token proxy

- Client-side extrapolation from last two known positions (capped at 200ms)
  smooths movement between 15hz network updates to 60fps rendering
- Input heartbeat every 200ms protects against lost unreliable packets
- Player timeout: server removes players after 60 seconds of no input
- Calls API token no longer exposed to clients — all API calls proxied
  through /api/calls/* endpoint on the game server
- Deploy script uses wrangler's Dockerfile path for automatic registry
  auth (Docker tokens expire quickly) with ARG cache-busting

  the signup trigger. Upsert was causing 400 errors with RLS.

  state that hasn't re-rendered yet.

Harden error handling, add session retry, backoff, and usability features

- Fix all silent error discards across client.go, crypto.go, commands.go, db.go
- Change rsaEncrypt to return error instead of silently skipping failed chunks
- Add MAC address validation for block/unblock/alias commands
- Fix alias file permissions from 0644 to 0600, warn on world-readable config
- Refactor Request() into requestOnce() with auto-retry on -40401/-40403 session expiry
- Add exponential backoff with max 10 consecutive failures for poll/monitor/watch
- Fix integer overflow: totalDown/totalUp now int64, report interval no longer hardcoded
- Deterministic output: sort wireless bands and alias MAC listing
- Fix runPurge to delete from all 4 tables (was only deleting bandwidth_samples)
- Add selective purge with --days N and --before YYYY-MM-DD flags
- Add auto-prune warning at 80% DB capacity, auto-prune at 90%
- Add --verbose/-v flag for request debug logging to stderr
- Add setup test-connect after saving config
- Add crypto_test.go with AES and RSA round-trip tests

Fix event loop blocking by switching to async Anthropic client

The synchronous anthropic.Anthropic() client was blocking the asyncio
event loop during background job processing, preventing FastAPI from
returning the job_id response. Switched all calls to
anthropic.AsyncAnthropic() with proper await, so the event loop stays
free while Claude processes in the background.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: split generate stage into analysis + transform phases (#15)

Replace the monolithic `generate_module_ast` + `process_statement_ast`
with a two-phase architecture:

1. `analyze_module` / `analyze_statement` — read-only pass that walks
   the original AST, determines a `StatementAction` for each statement,
   and collects exports/imports/star-exports directly into `GenerateAcc`
   without cloning any AST nodes.

2. Transform loop in `generate_module_ast` — selectively clones only
   the statements that survive tree-shaking. For unwrapped exports,
   clones only the inner declaration instead of the full export wrapper.

3. `prune_unused_imports` — extracted as a standalone function from the
   inline import-pruning logic.

Key improvement: eliminates the full-body `clone_in_with_semantic_ids`
that previously cloned every statement upfront, even those that would
be immediately discarded by tree-shaking.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement ChromaDB ingestion pipeline (T1.1.1, T1.1.2, T1.2.1)

CORE INGESTION FUNCTIONS:

✅ add_to_life_brain() - Single document ingestion
  - Validate required metadata fields (47-field schema)
  - Validate text self-containment (>100 chars, standalone readable)
  - Run conflict_check() from truth_engine
  - Handle CONFLICT/SOFT/ENRICHMENT/SAFE statuses
  - Upsert to ChromaDB collection
  - Comprehensive logging + error handling

✅ batch_ingest() - Bulk Q&A pair insertion (100+ docs)
  - Process each pair through add_to_life_brain()
  - Collect conflicts separately (for user review)
  - Return summary: {inserted, skipped, conflicts, success_rate}
  - Graceful error handling (skip on validation errors)
  - Per-document error tracking

✅ validate_document_batch() - Pre-ingestion validation
  - Check required metadata fields
  - Validate question/answer text quality
  - Detect duplicate questions within batch
  - Return (valid_ids, invalid_docs_with_reasons)
  - Comprehensive logging for debugging

CONFLICT INTEGRATION:
- Calls conflict_check() from truth_engine module
- Handles all conflict statuses:
  - HARD CONFLICT (>0.6): Blocks, raises ValueError
  - SOFT CONFLICT (0.3-0.6): Warns, logs, proceeds
  - ENRICHMENT (0.1-0.3): Auto-updates existing
  - SAFE (<0.1): Proceeds normally

LOGGING:
- Debug: Validation passed, conflict check results
- Warning: Soft conflicts, batch skips
- Error: Validation failures, ingestion errors
- Info: Successful ingestion, batch summary

This is the FOUNDATION for all downstream work. Everything depends on reliable ingestion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: life-history
repository.description: life history
